summary_en: FY3/26 final dividend raised from 23 yen/share to 24 yen/share (+1 yen). Annual dividend raised from 45 yen/share to 46 yen/share (+1 yen). Increase reflects strong real estate and aviation segment performance, reduced credit losses in overseas customer segment (US operations), and earnings accretion from consolidated subsidiary fiscal year-end change. Payout ratio 40%+ per 2023–2025 medium-term plan policy.
title_en: Notice of Revision to FY2026 March Term Final Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief:
Mitsubishi HC Capital announced a revision to its FY2026 March term dividend forecast on 15 May 2026. The final (期末) dividend is being increased from the previously forecast 23 yen per share to 24 yen per share, a raise of 1 yen. The annual dividend is therefore revised upward from 45 yen per share to 46 yen per share, also a 1 yen increase.

The hike follows better-than-expected earnings for the fiscal year. Strong performance in the real estate and aviation segments, a substantial reduction in credit-related losses in the overseas customer segment (US operations), and earnings accretion from a consolidated subsidiary's fiscal year-end change collectively caused full-year net profit to exceed the prior forecast. The company's base capital allocation policy targets a payout ratio of 40% or higher under its 2023–2025 medium-term plan. The increase aligns with this target and reflects the company's commitment to continuous and stable shareholder returns principally via dividend. The dividend remains subject to approval by the board of directors at its meeting scheduled for 20 May 2026.
